steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 2 makes a general claim: Siletz stories support the researchers’ belief about the shipwreck. Sentence 3 then names something specific those stories describe—beeswax washing ashore. The third sentence is an instance of the general claim, so the transition must introduce an example, not cause, contrast, or similarity.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘For this reason,’: “For this reason” signals that the beeswax stories were caused by the claim about support, which makes no sense. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘For example,’: “For example” introduces a specific instance of the general claim that Siletz stories support the researchers’ belief. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘However,’: “However” signals contrast, but the beeswax stories agree with, not contradict, the preceding claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Likewise,’: “Likewise” signals similarity between two parallel items, but the third sentence is not a parallel idea—it is a specific illustration. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
